formatDefinitions.displayName: Complete with words from the box

exercises.title: A: Word Formation (Gap-Fill)
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Over the last decade, podcasts have completely transformed the daily commute. Professionals who might once have been typical (1) ...... surfers – endlessly scrolling through social media feeds – have started (2) ...... listen instead. Many regular commuters have completely (3) ...... from background music playlists and tuned into educational programmes. When presented with the choice (4) ...... meaningless background noise and genuinely stimulating content, it seems the majority opt for substance over distraction. Listening to a well-crafted podcast (5) ...... a deep need for connection and learning that aimless browsing simply cannot. Anyone at a loose (6) ...... on a long train journey now reaches for earphones rather than a glossy magazine. Hosts typically hang (7) ...... after recording to discuss episodes informally, and this adds authenticity to their brand. Since the format has (8) ...... off across the globe, listeners have been only (9) ...... happy to subscribe and recommend their favourite shows to friends and colleagues alike. We are witnessing a quiet audio revolution – or, for (10) ...... of a more precise term, a genuine renaissance of the spoken word.
items.answer: couch, to, switched, between, satisfies, end, out, taken, too, lack
acceptedAnswers: couch, to, switched, between, satisfies, end, out, taken, too, lack

exercises.title: B: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
'I've made up my mind – I really want to move to the countryside,' said Marco. **heart**
Marco says ...... to the countryside.
items.answer: he has set his heart on moving
acceptedAnswers: he has set his heart on moving

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is determined to do, so the correct answer is "he has set his heart on moving".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "he has set his heart on moving", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
She reminded us that renewing our passports before the trip was the only possibility. **but**
'You ...... your passports before the trip,' she said.
items.answer: have no option but to renew
acceptedAnswers: have no option but to renew

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no choice, so the correct answer is "have no option but to renew".

wrongOptions.no option but to renew: This form misses the auxiliary — present perfect needs have/has + past participle.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "have no option but to renew", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence:
'Does Maria keep up with the latest fashion trends?' asked Liam. **interest**
Liam asked me ...... fashion trends.
items.answer: if Maria took an interest in
acceptedAnswers: if Maria took an interest in

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is reported question, so the correct answer is "if Maria took an interest in".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "if Maria took an interest in", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Indirect speech keeps statement word order — do not invert subject and auxiliary.

items.sentence:
'Now that the project is finally finished, I'm going to have some fun,' said Naomi. **hair**
Naomi said she ...... now that the project was finally finished.
items.answer: was going to let her hair down
acceptedAnswers: was going to let her hair down

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is relax and enjoy, so the correct answer is "was going to let her hair down".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "was going to let her hair down", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence:
As the old tram finally stopped at the end of the line, passengers began to collect their belongings. **rest**
As the old tram finally ...... at the end of the line, passengers began to collect their belongings.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is come to a stop, so the correct answer is "came to a rest".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "came to a rest", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
Gwen remarked that Daniel rarely arrives late. **like**
'It ...... late,' said Gwen.
items.answer: is not like Daniel to arrive
acceptedAnswers: is not like Daniel to arrive

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is unusual for someone, so the correct answer is "is not like Daniel to arrive".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "is not like Daniel to arrive", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence:
'I'd rather not commit to anything specific – let's just go with the flow,' said Finn. **ear**
Finn suggested ...... and just going with the flow.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is decide as you go, so the correct answer is "playing it by ear".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "playing it by ear", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
I made a snap decision to book a weekend trip and now I regret it entirely. **whim**
I ...... and now I regret it entirely.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is impulsive decision, so the correct answer is "booked it on a whim".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "booked it on a whim", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: C: Word Formation (Root Word Given)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Rosa does have a ...... (TEND) to procrastinate whenever she is under pressure.
items.explanation: Noun from TEND → a **tendency** to procrastinate.

items.sentence: Nobody should receive ...... (PREFER) access to public services simply because of their social status.
She was handed the ...... (ENVY) responsibility of informing the entire staff that the office would be relocated.
The spokesperson tried to ...... (PLAY) the severity of the data breach, calling it a minor technical issue.
Shopping for furniture with Lucas is exhausting – he's incredibly ...... (CHOICE) and never settles on anything quickly.
The pasta was left on the hob for too long and was slightly ...... (DO) by the time we finally sat down to eat.
Despite not being particularly ...... (SPORT) , she signed up for a beginners' cycling class last month.
An ...... (MOTIVE) employee is unlikely to produce their best work, regardless of the resources available to them.

exercises.title: D: Phrasal Verbs / Expressions (Gap-Fill)
exercises.instructions: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form. You will not need one of the words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form. You will not need one of the words.

items.sentence: My family has never really ....... in for extreme sports, though my brother tried surfing once.
The documentary ...... on the daily lives of migratory birds in the Arctic.
items.explanation: Focus on → documentary **centres** on daily lives.

items.sentence: Remember to be sensible tonight and try not to ...... into any mischief, please.
Abstract art never appealed to me before, but it's slowly ...... on me since I visited that gallery.
Would it be possible for you to ...... me up from the station at around seven this evening?
items.explanation: Collect by car → **pick** me up from the station.

items.sentence: The new thriller ....... with a gripping scene set in an abandoned lighthouse on the Scottish coast.
items.explanation: Begin with → thriller **opens** with a gripping scene.

items.sentence: The coach asked us all to ........ up properly before the training session began.
Would you please stop ...... around and focus on what we're supposed to be doing?

items.sentenceBefore: After a long day of back-to-back meetings, she needed some time to
items.sentenceAfter: before she could even think about cooking dinner.
